title: camassia quamash subsp. walpolei
intro: Camassia quamash subsp. walpolei, also known as the Camas, is a perennial plant species native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. It has been used by indigenous peoples for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's roots, leaves, and flowers have been used to treat various health issues, such as wounds, fever, and digestive problems, with potential benefits including reduced inflammation, improved wound healing, and relief from respiratory issues. Traditionally, the plant's parts were used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and poultices, and it is still used today in some herbal remedies, although its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
